title: Visiting the San Francisco Women Artists Gallery - Splash Magazines Worldwide
content: # Visiting the San Francisco Women Artists Gallery. San Francisco Women Artist Gallery reception, Courtesy of the gallery. Spending some time in the gallery it became clear to me that this is a hidden treasure in the Sunset district. The gallery is small but vibrant with energy and the work of talented artists. San Francisco Women Artist Gallery, photo: Jessica Levant. There is also an “Artists Salon” behind the main gallery where members or organizations can rent a wall or the whole room for a month to display their own art. Founded as the “Sketch Club” in 1887, San Francisco Women Artists began as an independent group of women who met to share and critique one another’s artwork. Their mission continues to support women artists, however their membership is not limited to women, but to any artist who supports women artists. Reception at San Francisco Women Artists Gallery, Courtesy of the gallery.
